task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a channel named 'Ops_Dashboard_Generator' in the NextGen Connect Administrator interface (Channels view or Dashboard).

task_completion.description: Configure the channel to poll every 30 seconds
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Source connector settings (e.g., JavaScript Reader or HTTP Listener/Reader) showing a polling interval or frequency set to 30 seconds (or 30000 ms).

task_completion.description: Configure the API request to the statistics endpoint with required credentials and headers
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an HTTP Sender, JavaScript Reader, or script step configured to request 'https://localhost:8443/api/channels/statistics'. It must show authentication (admin/admin) and the headers 'X-Requested-With: OpenAPI' and 'Accept: application/json'.

task_completion.description: Transform the JSON statistics into an HTML table with specific columns
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a transformer, script, or code block that parses the JSON response and constructs an HTML table. The code must explicitly include table headers for 'Channel Name', 'Received', 'Queued', and 'Error'.

task_completion.description: Write the final HTML output to /var/www/html/dashboard.html
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a File Writer destination or a script step configured to write or output the generated HTML to the file path '/var/www/html/dashboard.html'.

task_completion.description: Ensure data for 'Sim_ADT_Inbound' and 'Sim_Lab_Results' is included in the output
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal output (e.g., cat /var/www/html/dashboard.html) or a browser view of the dashboard showing the HTML table populated with rows for 'Sim_ADT_Inbound' and 'Sim_Lab_Results'.

integrity.description: The agent used the NextGen Connect Administrator GUI to create the channel
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing interaction with the NextGen Connect Administrator Java application, rather than the agent just writing a standalone Python or Bash script to bypass the application.

integrity.description: The HTML file was generated by the channel, not manually typed
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the agent did not simply open a text editor (like nano or vim) and manually type out the HTML table with hardcoded values for the simulation channels.

integrity.description: The channel interacts with the actual API endpoint
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the URL 'https://localhost:8443/api/channels/statistics' is actually used in the channel configuration, rather than the agent hardcoding a mock JSON response.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The API endpoint is 'https://localhost:8443/api/channels/statistics'. The output file must be exactly '/var/www/html/dashboard.html'. The simulation channels that must appear in the final HTML dashboard are 'Sim_ADT_Inbound' and 'Sim_Lab_Results'.
